What Are Claude Code Hooks? Auto-Run Lint, Block rm -rf, Inject Context on SessionStart — A Complete Guide

When you're developing with Claude Code, do you find yourself constantly telling it to run Prettier again? Or worrying that one day it'll accidentally fire off rm -rf? This post walks through how Claude Code Hooks work — when events like PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, Notification, and Stop fire, how to set up matcher and command in settings.json, how to block actions with exit code 2 — and runs through practical hooks: auto-format, blocking rm -rf, reinjecting project context after Compact, Mac desktop notifications, plus prompt / agent hook types and /hooks debugging.
Claude Code Routines: How to Set Up /schedule (and Why It Pushes to claude/ Instead of main)

I scheduled a task with Claude Code's /schedule, came back to the repo next day, and asked myself: I told it to push to main, why are there a bunch of claude/xxx branches instead? This post walks through Claude Code's three scheduling options (Routines, Desktop scheduled tasks, /loop), how /schedule works on the CLI, Routines' three trigger types (Schedule, API, GitHub event), custom cron rules, the 1-hour minimum interval — and most importantly, why pushes land on claude/-prefixed branches and how to flip "Allow unrestricted branch pushes" to fix that confusing behavior.
What Are Claude Code's Built-in Tools Doing? Understand How It Picks Tools — Then Write Better Prompts

This post is a quick chat about Claude Code's built-in Tools. If you've used Claude Code, you've probably noticed something — sometimes when you ask it to read a file, it just opens it with Read. Other times when you ask "what components does this project have," it goes off and runs Glob and Grep. And occasionally, it takes a detour through Bash's cat or grep, which is confusing because the built-in tools are right there. Claude actually has its own logic for picking tools — this post walks through what built-in Tools Claude Code has, how it decides which one to use, and how to reverse-engineer its tool usage to fix your prompts.
From 3 to 100: Passing isitagentready's AI Agent Readiness Test

I ran my blog through Cloudflare's isitagentready.com and got a brutal 3/100. Here's how I fixed every single item, one by one, all the way to a perfect score — code, screenshots, and the gotchas I hit along the way.
AI Agent Isn't One Thing Anymore — Understanding Coding Agents and Other AI Agent Types

Back when I wrote about AI terminology, AI Agent was used as a catch-all term for systems that can understand a task on their own, break it into steps, and use tools to get things done. But here in 2026, the term AI Agent has started to splinter into more specific subcategories — and the most mature one is the Coding Agent (some people call it Code Agent). This post walks through what these categories actually mean and how they differ.
Which AI Coding Tool Should You Pick in 2026? Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code vs Kiro vs Antigravity

AI Coding tools are everywhere in 2026 — GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Kiro, and Google Antigravity. Which one should you actually pick? This post compares all five across features, pricing, real-world use cases, and Agent capabilities so you can find the one that fits your workflow.
Don't Want to Use the Lobster (OpenClaw)? Try Claude Code Channels Instead!

Have you been using OpenClaw (the "lobster") for remote computer control? Claude Code actually has a similar feature called "Channels" that lets you push messages from Telegram or Discord into a running Claude Code Session for Claude to handle. This post walks through what Channels are, how to set them up, and real-world use cases.
Claude Cowork Dispatch and Remote Control — Control Your AI Agent From Your Phone

Ever thought about controlling the AI Agent on your computer from your phone? Anthropic recently launched two big features — "Dispatch" and "Remote Control" — that let you remotely control Claude Cowork and Claude Code from your phone. Even when you're away from your computer, you can assign tasks, monitor progress, and come back to a finished result. This post walks through the differences between the two features, how to set them up, and real-world use cases.
Want to Develop With Claude Code? This Guide Takes You From Beginner to Advanced

As a developer, you've probably heard of AI Agent dev tools like Gemini, Codex, Claude Code, and so on. Most developers these days subscribe to a few AI Agent tools to speed up their dev workflow. This post picks one AI Agent tool — Claude Code — and walks through how to use it for development. There may be acceleration techniques and usage concepts here you don't know yet, and this post includes practical examples so you can get up to speed faster.
What Is Git Worktree? The Secret to Running Multiple AI Agents in One Project

Most developers are using AI Agents to help build things now, but real projects usually have multiple features in flight at once. So the question becomes: can one project only run one AI Agent at a time? This post walks through how Git Worktree lets you run several AI Agents in the same project — at the same time.
